hero.tagline: Authentic Bolivian Cuisine in the USA
hero.description: We're bringing the vibrant flavors of Cochabamba, Bolivia to the United States. Family recipes passed down through generations, now serving you from our food truck.

menu.title: Our Menu
menu.subtitle: Authentic flavors from the heart of Bolivia

saltenas.name: Salteñas
saltenas.description: Traditional Bolivian empanadas filled with savory meat stew, sweet potatoes, and olives. A perfect morning snack.

piqueMacho.name: Pique Macho
piqueMacho.description: Cochabamba's signature dish! Grilled beef strips over fries, topped with onions, tomatoes, peppers, and llajua sauce.

anticuchos.name: Anticuchos
anticuchos.description: Marinated beef heart skewers grilled to perfection, served with potatoes and spicy peanut sauce.

silpancho.name: Silpancho
silpancho.description: Thin breaded beef cutlet over rice and potatoes, topped with fried egg, onions, tomatoes, and llajua.

tucumanas.name: Tucumanas
tucumanas.description: Crispy fried empanadas filled with seasoned ground beef, perfect with our house sauces.

yucaFrita.name: Yuca Frita
yucaFrita.description: Golden fried cassava root, crispy outside and soft inside. Served with llajua or cheese sauce.

platanosFritos.name: Plátanos Fritos
platanosFritos.description: Sweet plantains fried to caramelized perfection. A beloved side dish.

choripan.name: Choripán
choripan.description: Grilled chorizo in crusty bread with chimichurri and grilled onions.

location.title: Find Our Truck
location.subtitle: We're always on the move - catch us at these spots!

catering.title: Catering & Events
catering.subtitle: Bring authentic Bolivian flavors to your next event!
catering.description: From intimate gatherings to large festivals, we bring the authentic taste of Bolivia to your event. Our mobile kitchen can serve anywhere from 20 to 500 guests.

about.title: Our Story
about.subtitle: From Cochabamba to your neighborhood
about.story1: Our journey began in the valleys of Cochabamba, Bolivia, where food is more than sustenance—it's a celebration of family, tradition, and community. Growing up in Tarata, a small town known for its vibrant markets and street food culture, recipes were passed down through generations like precious heirlooms.
about.story2: After moving to the United States, we missed the authentic flavors of home—the sizzling anticuchos from street vendors, the warm salteñas enjoyed for breakfast, and the hearty pique macho shared with friends. So we decided to bring those flavors to you.
about.story3: Today, our food truck carries on the traditions of Punata, Tolata, and all the beautiful towns of Cochabamba. Every dish is made with the same techniques and passion that our abuelas taught us, using recipes that have been perfected over decades.

gallery.subtitle: A taste of Bolivia in every bite
